markdown.markdown:
My teaching is committedly participatory. My students one way or another are required to prepare before the session to be able to ask and respond to questions. I conceive my role as prompting knowledge but especially critical thinking; my interventions during teaching are, therefore, more questions than answers. I do not expect my students to know the answers but to be able to think with and against me. I believe this method helps instilling fundamental academic and work skills: tolerance to frustration and uncertainty, and responsibility. Two of my undergraduate professors influenced this style: [Luis Rodríguez Collao](https://editorial.tirant.com/cl/autorList/luis-rodriguez-collao-613268) and [Enzo Solari Alliende](https://www.pucv.cl/uuaa/centro-derecho/academicos/nucleo/enzo-solari-alliende). I owe them ability to think.


I currently supervise undergraduate students in the course of [Statistics and Methods]().

During Michaelmas I was the teaching assistant of [Introduction to Quantitative Analysis](https://clanfear.github.io/ioc_iqa/_site/index.html). I was also an invited lecturer for Criminological Research Methods in the class of Critiquing Quantitative Research. Both for the Master's in Criminology

## 2024-2025 University of Cambridge

I supervised undergraduate students in the course of Foundations in Criminology and Criminal Justice.

I was the teaching assistant of Introduction to Quantitative Analysis and an invited lecturer for Criminological Research Methods in the class of using LLMs, both for the Master's in Criminology.

## Before 2024

**Legal Skills** · Universidad Adolfo Ibáñez · Lecturer · 2022

**Theory and Philosophy of Law** · Pontificia Universidad Católica de Valparaíso · Guest Lecturer in Philosophy of Criminal Law and Feminist Theory · 2018–2020

**Criminology and Criminal Policy** · Universidad de O'Higgins · TA · 2021

**Criminal Policy** · Pontificia Universidad Católica de Chile · Postgraduate Diploma in Criminology · TA · 2021

**Qualitative Methods for Social Research** · Pontificia Universidad Católica de Chile · Postgraduate Diploma · TA · 2021

**Legal Theory and Philosophy of Law** · Pontificia Universidad Católica de Valparaíso · TA · 2019.

**Criminal Law and Criminal Justice** · Pontificia Universidad Católica de Valparaíso · TA · 2016–2019
